pet.name: Saw
pet.special_needs: Needs more oxygen than other fishes..
pet.characteristics: High-oxygen fish typically include active species such as goldfish, plecos, or fast-swimming cyprinids that require robust aeration and water circulation. Care involves maintaining dissolved oxygen levels through air pumps, powerheads, or surface agitation; regular water changes (25–50% weekly) to prevent oxygen depletion; cool or moderate water temperatures depending on species; a varied diet of quality pellets and occasional live/frozen foods; and monitoring for signs of oxygen stress (gasping at surface, lethargy). Tank size should be generous to support activity and gas exchange. Enrichment includes plants, hiding spots, and substrate for foraging. Handling is minimal; observation for behavioral changes and water parameters (pH, ammonia, nitrite, nitrate) is the primary monitoring method.
pet.species_profile_summary: Lifespan varies widely by specific species and water quality; high-oxygen fish generally thrive 3–10 years in well-aerated, maintained captive environments.

age_context.summary: Lifespan varies widely by specific species and water quality; high-oxygen fish generally thrive 3–10 years in well-aerated, maintained captive environments.

goal: Create a complete pet care plan with daily, weekly, monthly tasks, reminders, and safe condition-aware guidance.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

retrieved_passages.title: Medication Routine and Safety
retrieved_passages.content: Medication-related tasks in a home routine should focus on reminders, documentation, and observation, not dosage changes. A planning assistant can remind the owner to give medication exactly as already prescribed and to log whether the dose was completed.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: A senior pet plan should prioritize consistency over intensity. When time is limited, essential care such as feeding, hydration, medication reminders, and mobility support should be scheduled before optional enrichment.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: When the day's schedule is tight, a planner should preserve high-priority essentials first and then fill remaining time with short enrichment or monitoring tasks that still fit the available budget.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Owners should follow the veterinary feeding plan already prescribed and avoid changing food or supplement amounts without veterinary guidance. If a pet refuses multiple meals or shows sudden decline, the safe action is to contact a veterinarian.

raw_recommendations.name: Daily Feeding
raw_recommendations.notes: Provide quality pellets appropriate for high-oxygen fish species. Observe appetite and food consumption as part of daily monitoring.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent feeding is essential for senior pet health and provides opportunity to observe behavioral changes and appetite patterns.

raw_recommendations.name: Water Parameter Observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe Saw for signs of oxygen stress (gasping at surface, lethargy). Monitor tank for adequate aeration and water circulation. Note any behavioral changes.
raw_recommendations.rationale: High-oxygen fish require consistent monitoring of dissolved oxygen levels and behavioral indicators. Daily observation creates a record of health status and catches early signs of distress.

raw_recommendations.name: Air Pump and Aeration Check
raw_recommendations.notes: Verify air pump is running, check for adequate surface agitation and water circulation. Ensure powerhead or aeration device is functioning properly.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Saw requires more oxygen than typical fish. Daily verification of aeration equipment is critical to prevent oxygen depletion and stress.

raw_recommendations.name: Weekly Water Change (25–50%)
raw_recommendations.notes: Perform 25–50% water change to prevent oxygen depletion and maintain water quality. Monitor pH, ammonia, nitrite, and nitrate levels if possible.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Regular water changes are essential for high-oxygen fish to maintain dissolved oxygen and prevent accumulation of waste products that consume oxygen.

raw_recommendations.name: Tank Temperature Check
raw_recommendations.notes: Verify tank temperature is appropriate for Saw's species (cool to moderate range). Record temperature if tracking water parameters.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Temperature affects dissolved oxygen levels and overall health. Consistent monitoring supports senior pet stability and prevents stress.

raw_recommendations.name: Enrichment and Habitat Observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe Saw interacting with plants, hiding spots, and substrate. Ensure enrichment items are in place and not causing stress or blocking water circulation.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Enrichment supports mental stimulation and natural behaviors. Observation during enrichment time provides additional behavioral monitoring data.

raw_recommendations.name: Monthly Water Parameter Testing
raw_recommendations.notes: Test and record pH, ammonia, nitrite, and nitrate levels. Document results to track water quality trends over time.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Comprehensive water parameter testing identifies trends and potential issues affecting oxygen availability and overall tank health for a senior, high-oxygen fish.

raw_recommendations.name: Equipment Maintenance Review
raw_recommendations.notes: Inspect air pump, powerhead, filter, and tubing for wear or blockages. Clean air stone if present. Ensure all aeration equipment is functioning optimally.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Regular maintenance of aeration equipment is critical for a high-oxygen fish. Equipment failure directly impacts Saw's ability to access adequate dissolved oxygen.
